properties.evidence: tendon xanthomas develop in childhood, LDL levels normal to high
associated with premature diffuse vascular disease, male predominance, Palmar xanthomas are pathognomonic

Results in elevated chylomicrons, which carry dietary fat; chylomicrons are generally not present after an overnight fast, so a creamy looking plasma in a fasting specimen should be a clue to the diagnosis, especially if seen in young children

Results in elevated chylomicrons, which carry dietary fat; chylomicrons are generally not present after an overnight fast, so a creamy looking plasma in a fasting specimen should be a clue to the diagnosis, especially if seen in young children
extremely high triglycerides can lead to pancreatitis
This apolipoprotein is an activator of LPL; its absence causes a clinical picture identical to LPL deficiency
Main defect is overproduction of VLDL triglycerides by the liver
